question_title: Will the interest in “caroline kennedy” change between 2026-03-02 and 2026-03-11 according to Google Trends?
summary: March 2 is at 100 — the peak of the Google Trends range for "caroline kennedy." The spike was driven by a confluence of Caroline Kennedy's rare CBS Sunday Morning interview (March 1), the ongoing FX Love Story series, and Jack Schlossberg's congressional campaign. Historical decay patterns from this search term show values dropping to ~50% of peak within 9 days. With the ±3 threshold being negligible compared to the expected 45-70 point decline, "Decreases" is overwhelmingly likely. For "Doesn't change," March 11 would need to be 97-103 (near-impossible absent a major new catalyst). For "Increases," March 11 would need to exceed 103 (requires an even bigger spike than the current peak). The Love Story series provides a sustained interest floor (~30-50 range) but not enough to prevent a large decline from the peak.

factors.description: March 2 is at 100 (peak of the entire range). Google Trends relative scaling means March 11 cannot exceed 100 unless a new peak emerges between Mar 3-10 that rescales everything. March 11 would need value 97+ to avoid 'Decreases', which requires near-peak search volume — extremely unlikely.

Historical decay pattern: previous spikes in this term decayed from 51→27 in 4 days and 60→29 in 5 days. Computational analysis shows ~50% retention at 9 days from peak. Expected March 11 value of ~30-55, representing a 45-70 point decline — far exceeding the ±3 threshold.

The March 1-2 spike was driven by a specific one-time catalyst: Caroline Kennedy's rare CBS Sunday Morning interview endorsing son Jack Schlossberg's congressional campaign. This won't repeat on March 11.

Love Story FX series has weekly episodes through March 26, providing a sustained interest floor. But previous episode-only spikes reached only 51-60 (relative), well below the 100 peak driven by the CBS interview confluence. Partially offsets decline speed but doesn't prevent it.

Jack Schlossberg's congressional campaign and ongoing Kennedy family news could produce unexpected spikes. No specific events scheduled for March 11, but black swan events (health emergency, major political statement) are always possible.
For 'Doesn't change', March 11 would need to be 97-103 — nearly identical search volume to the peak day. This requires a major new catalyst on exactly March 11, which is extremely unlikely.

reasoning:
**Forecast: Decreases (90%)**

The key finding: "caroline kennedy" is at 100 on March 2 — the absolute peak of the Google Trends range, driven by a confluence of her rare CBS Sunday Morning interview (March 1), the FX Love Story series, and son Jack Schlossberg's congressional campaign.

**Why "Decreases" dominates (90%):**
- At 100, values can only go down (or stay flat) in relative scaling
- Previous spikes in this term decayed ~50% within 9 days (51→27, 60→29)
- The CBS interview was a one-time catalyst that won't repeat
- March 11 would need to be 97+ to avoid "Decreases" — essentially requiring near-peak search volume

**Why not "Doesn't change" (7%):**
- Would require March 11 in the 97-103 range — nearly identical to peak interest
- Only possible with a major new catalyst on exactly that day

**Why not "Increases" (3%):**
- Would require March 11 > 103, meaning even higher search volume than the current peak
- Would need a day between Mar 3-10 to become the new peak (rescaling March 2 down) AND March 11 to exceed the rescaled March 2 by >3
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will the interest in “caroline kennedy” change between 2026-03-02 and 2026-03-11 according to Google Trends?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42380)
